evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup qualification - Wikipedia
evidences.content: ## Confederation qualification ### AFC Main article: 2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC) "2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)") On 1 August 2022, the Asian Football Confederation Executive Committee approved the qualification format for Asia's road to the 2026 World Cup, as well as the 2027 AFC Asian Cup, in preparation for the eight direct spots and the single intercontinental play-off slot allocated to the AFC by FIFA following the expansion of the FIFA World Cup to 48 teams. The draw for the first round was held on 27 July 2023 at the AFC House in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. The draw for the second round took place two hours later. The qualification structure was as follows: [...] ## Slot allocation On 30 March 2017, the Bureau of the FIFA Council (composed of the FIFA president and the presidents of each of the six confederations) proposed a slot allocation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The recommendation was submitted for ratification by the FIFA Council. On 9 May 2017, two days before the 67th FIFA Congress, the FIFA Council approved the slot allocation in a meeting in Manama, Bahrain. This included an intercontinental play-off tournament involving six teams to decide t...

evidences.title: World Cup 2026 Qualifiers: How the Expanded Format Is Shaping Global Football
evidences.content: ## What's new in the FIFA World Cup 26™ qualifying format? FIFA World Cup 26™ marks a major shift in the global structure of FIFA World Cup™ qualifying. For the first time, 48 teams will compete in the final tournament — up from 32 in previous editions. This expansion means more slots for each confederation, a larger pool of qualifiers, and a new intercontinental play-off tournament to determine the final two spots. The broader format is designed to boost global representation, giving more nations a genuine chance to reach the FIFA World Cup™. It also introduces an added layer of competition, with a new stage of semi-finals built into the play-off tournament, raising the stakes in the final phase of qualification. Here's a snapshot of how the 2026 format compares to 2022: [...] 1. Regional group stages. Each confederation organizes group stages where teams compete for top positions. Group size and structure differ, but this is the first hurdle in FIFA World Cup™ qualifying. 2. Automatic qualification for group winners. In most regions, group winners advance directly to FIFA World Cup 26™. Slot allocation varies by confederation. For example, CAF gets 9 direct spots, while CONMEB...

Furthermore, the CONMEBOL quota somewhat increases under Seeded set S2 if the sample is finished later. Hence, the FIFA policy of supporting emerging nations has had questionable results, and the overall quality of the 2026 FIFA World Cup will probably be lower due to the stronger presence of AFC and CAF countries. evidences.title: 48 Thoughts and Picks for 48 World Cup Teams
evidences.content: # Union Soccer Blog # 48 Thoughts and Picks for 48 World Cup Teams ### My far too long deep dive into the 2026 World Cup. Joe Tansey's avatar The 2026 FIFA World Cup is quite literally the biggest tournament in the event’s history. The expanded tournament provides an opportunity for lesser-known countries to have an experience on the international stage that they’ve never had before. That also opens up the possibility for a lot of lopsided results in the group stage. At least my ideal 48-team World Cup is a lot similar to how March Madness should play out. A handful of upsets early with one or two really good Cinderella stories, but at the end, chalk prevails. There’s really only six-to-eight countries that can actually win the whole damn thing. So away we go on the deep dive. [...] Share Union Soccer Blog Group J Argentina: Group J is another group with a very clear favorite in Argentina. Austria, Jordan and Algeria are most likely playing for second place. Argentina’s World Cup run is going to be dominated by Lionel Messi, but Julian Alvarez needs to have a strong tournament for the Albiceleste to go back-to-back. Of Alvarez’s 14 goals for Argentina, three came at the 2022 Wor...
